headline: Washington Protects Domestic Workers With Historic Bill of Rights
meta_description: Washington state has signed a Domestic Workers Bill of Rights into law, guaranteeing minimum wage, overtime, and legal protections for household workers.
summary_short: Washington state has enacted a landmark Domestic Workers Bill of Rights, establishing minimum wage, overtime pay, and legal protections for nannies and housekeepers.
body:
Washington state has taken a significant step toward worker dignity by enacting a comprehensive Domestic Workers Bill of Rights. The law extends crucial protections to nannies, housekeepers, caregivers, and other household workers—occupations that have long operated in a legal gray zone.

The legislation guarantees minimum wage protections, overtime pay, and requires written contracts so workers know their rights and expectations in advance. Beyond wages, the law includes privacy protections and legal safeguards, ensuring that domestic workers have recourse if they face exploitation or unfair treatment.

Domestic work is essential to countless families and communities, yet those who perform it have historically lacked the basic labor protections most workers take for granted. This bill recognizes the value of their labor and their dignity as workers. By bringing household employment into the framework of formal labor law, Washington sets a model that other states and regions can follow.
